description: Remove @simulated annotations from all 12 agent-runtime pilot files and ensure they all properly delegate to this.aiEngine rather than using any hardcoded if/else rule-based logic. Most pilots already call this.aiEngine.infer() but still carry @simulated/@NAIVE comments. Also rename any remaining 'analyzeWithAI' private methods to clearly indicate they use the real AI engine (not simulation), and remove any dead rule-based code paths that existed before the AI integration.
steps: For each of the 12 pilot files at packages/agent-runtime/src/agents/{code,content,email,finance,health,learning,meeting,research,schedule,shopping,social,travel}-pilot.ts: Remove the @simulated JSDoc comment block (the 4-line comment starting with '/**' and ending with '*/'). Remove the Classification: NAIVE line. Remove the 'Production path:' line. These are outdated since the pilots now use aiEngine.

In each pilot file, verify there is no hardcoded if/else logic that returns fake responses without calling aiEngine. If any pilot has a fallback path that returns hardcoded data without attempting AI first, restructure so AI is always attempted first with the hardcoded path only used in the catch block.
Ensure all 12 pilot files compile cleanly with pnpm turbo typecheck --filter=agent-runtime
acceptance_criteria: No file in packages/agent-runtime/src/agents/ contains '@simulated' annotation
All 12 pilots call this.aiEngine.infer() or this.aiEngine.classify() in their execute method
pnpm turbo typecheck passes for packages/agent-runtime
